morning.notes: Skipped — one of the boys had an early thing. Noticed the difference in afternoon focus. Logging this because the correlation is consistent.

deep_work.notes: The question is almost there: 'What does it take to build a meaningful, skill-based career in a world that structurally rewards busyness over depth — and why do so many smart, motivated people fail at this even when they know better?' The second clause is new. I think it's the book.
deep_work.question_statement_progress: Third draft — sharing with editor this week

research.notes: Conference review came back — major revisions. Expected. The boundary conditions section needs more formal treatment. Added to next quarter's deep work budget.

new_yorker.notes: Editor notes came back: 'the organizational section is the most interesting part and also the most underdeveloped.' She's right. Revising.

teaching.notes: Semester hits its density peak this week. Three seminars, one undergraduate class, two office hours blocks. I knew this was coming and still find the week slightly airless.
teaching.note: Held the two-per-month limit. One podcast, one talk. Both scheduled before the semester, both short. The limit is working.

family.notes: Shutdown at 5pm — only four days this week, not five. The missed day was the conference review email arriving at 4:50. I answered it. I shouldn't have.

at_risk: Conference paper revision needs scheduling — goes on Q2 list
One shutdown missed — watch this week
note: The 4:50pm email. I knew what it was before I opened it, and I opened it anyway. The protocol exists for this exact situation. I will not do it again this week.
